title: Cascadia Pharmacy
text:
Healthcare / Pharmacy Analytics




Turning messy federal public data — CMS Part D suppressed cells, CDC VaxView age-band mismatches — into a clean star schema and an interview-ready GLP-1 growth story.

text:

text:

TODO (Cowork — Business Problem): Frame through a CVS lens: Medicare Part D drug spend trends (especially GLP-1s / obesity drugs) and adult immunization coverage gaps matter directly to a pharmacy chain’s formulary strategy and immunization programs. What would a CVS analytics leader want to answer from this data? (E.g., “Which drugs drove the largest spend increases? Which states have the lowest flu vaccination rates?”)

title: Cascadia Pharmacy
text:
Architecture




Cascadia end-to-end data platform



Source layer: CascadiaRx SQL Server star schema — 5 dimension tables + 2 fact tables.

fact_partd_drug_spend (339 rows, grain: drug × year 2020–2024, CMS Part D data)
fact_immunization_coverage (24,596 rows, grain: vaccine × geography × demographic × season, CDC VaxView)

Medallion pipeline: Bronze → Silver → Gold notebooks in Microsoft Fabric Lakehouse (Phase 2, in build).
Semantic model + report: Power BI — GLP-1 Growth Story page + Immunization Coverage & Access page (Phase 2, in build).

title: Cascadia Pharmacy
text:
Headline Skill: Messy Public-Data Cleaning


TODO (Cowork — Headline Skill): This is the CVS-interview-ready centerpiece. Draft 2–3 paragraphs covering: 1. CMS suppression handling — blank cells in the wide-format CSV mean “suppressed” (beneficiary count < 11), not zero. Explain how the pipeline preserves suppressed_flag=1 rather than zero-filling, and why this matters for accurate trend analysis. 2. Wide-to-long pivot — CMS data arrives with year columns (Tot_Spndng_2020 through Tot_Spndng_2024) on a single row per drug. Explain the pivot logic and drug classification approach. 3. CDC column mismatch + age-band normalization — FluVaxView uses non-standard column names and “NR †” for not-reportable cells. How the pipeline handles these without silently defaulting suppressed estimates to zero. Make it interview-conversational: “Here’s the dirt I found, here’s why it matters, here’s what I did.”

title: Cascadia Clothing
text:
Retail Analytics




The third domain in the Cascadia family — applying the same production-grade analytics architecture to retail customer and inventory data.

title: Cascadia Clothing
text:
Planned Scope

Domain: Retail fashion (direct-to-consumer e-commerce)
Business questions: RFM customer segmentation, inventory turnover by SKU, return rate analysis
Data: TBD — either synthetic or a suitable public e-commerce dataset
Headline skill: Customer lifetime value modeling / cohort analysis
Architecture: Same SQL Server → Fabric → Power BI pattern as Medical Devices and Pharmacy

title: Cascadia Clothing
text:
Why Three Domains?
The Cascadia portfolio is designed to demonstrate that the engineering skill — star schema design, medallion pipeline patterns, DAX semantic modeling — transfers across industries. Adding a retail domain alongside manufacturing and healthcare makes the case that this is architectural thinking, not just domain familiarity.
See the full Cascadia architecture →

title: The Cascadia Family
text:
Design Principles


Production patterns only Every table, index, and pipeline uses the same conventions you’d enforce on a real production system — surrogate keys, computed date_key columns, idempotent DDL, row-count validation scripts.


Real data where it matters CMS Medicare Part D spending and CDC VaxView immunization data are live federal public-domain datasets. Fabricated data is limited to the manufacturing simulation layer (volume, anonymity).


Repeatable builds Each domain ships a run_phaseN.ps1 script that rebuilds the SQL Server database from scratch — useful for demos, onboarding, and validating migrations.


Clean handoff documentation Every domain has an explainer/ doc describing schema decisions, data-cleaning choices, and interview talking points, so a reviewer can follow the reasoning without reading the code.

title: Cascadia Medical Devices
text:
Architecture
Every Cascadia domain uses the same end-to-end stack:




Cascadia end-to-end data platform



Source layer: CascadiaMES SQL Server star schema — 7 dimension tables + 3 fact tables (fact_production_event 5.93M rows, fact_downtime_event 6.5K rows, fact_quality_qn 41K rows, fact_order_fulfillment). Simulated MES data + NASA C-MAPSS engine sensor dataset (265K rows) for predictive maintenance.
Medallion pipeline: Bronze → Silver → Gold notebooks in Microsoft Fabric Lakehouse, implemented in PySpark.
Semantic model + report: Power BI dataset with DAX measures for OEE, MTBF, defect rate, and on-time delivery.
